behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed; unchanged files are not scanned
behaviors.justification: Tests the integration between GitHub API communication (which returns the changed files list) and file analysis logic. A unit test with mocked GitHub API could catch logic bugs, but would miss real API communication failures like pagination mishandling, race conditions between API response and analysis start, or error handling in the API layer. Integration testing with a mock GitHub server (or local test PR) ensures the behavior holds end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a mock GitHub server or local test PR with 5 changed files and 10 unchanged files. Run the lint step. Verify via logs/coverage that only the 5 changed files are analyzed and unchanged files are never opened or scanned.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic grouping logic: given a set of findings with severity fields, partition them into buckets. No I/O, no external dependencies, no non-determinism. Pure function logic can be tested in isolation by providing test findings with mixed severities.
behaviors.test_description: Create test findings with mixed severities: 3 errors, 2 warnings, 1 info. Pass to the grouping function. Verify the output groups them under error/warning/info headers with correct counts.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout
behaviors.justification: Performance and timeout behavior depends on real I/O speed (file read, parse, linting execution on actual files). Mocking file I/O would show completion in milliseconds and hide a real timeout failure. MOCK-HIDE check: a fully mocked test would pass even if the real implementation exceeds 10 minutes. Only real I/O can reveal whether the system meets the performance SLA.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test directory with 200 real files of realistic size (mix of small and large). Run the lint step end-to-end on a controlled system. Measure elapsed time. Verify completion within 10 minutes. Run multiple times to account for system variance and ensure consistent performance.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted
behaviors.justification: Testing subprocess error handling requires real subprocess invocation. A mocked linter could hide real subprocess failure modes: exit code handling, signal handling (SIGKILL, SIGSEGV), stderr/stdout buffering, process zombie states, or hanging processes. MOCK-HIDE check: mock subprocess that raises exception would always be caught; real subprocess that crashes with signal or hangs would not be caught by mock.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test directory with 10 files, one intentionally malformed (syntax error, binary junk, or corrupted content). Run the lint step. Verify the linter encounters the malformed file and crashes/errors, but error handling prevents pipeline failure. Verify results are posted for the remaining 9 analyzed files.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output
behaviors.justification: Deduplication is deterministic logic: given findings with (file, line, rule) keys, deduplicate by (file, line). No I/O, no external calls, no non-determinism. Pure comparison and filtering logic tested by providing duplicate findings.
behaviors.test_description: Create two overlapping linting rules (e.g., both flagging whitespace issues). Generate test findings where both rules flag the same issue on the same line. Run deduplication. Verify only one finding appears in the output for that (file, line) pair.
